description_long: This house features a combination of a rectangular brick main structure and an attached gabled extension with exposed wooden rafters. The main section is a tall, boxy volume clad in a uniform brick pattern, while the extension has a pitched roof with visible timber framing and large blue-tinted windows. The gabled end of the extension is almost entirely glazed, providing ample natural light and a modern aesthetic. The roof of the extension is partially open, revealing the wooden beams and the interior space below. Windows are symmetrically arranged on the extension, and the overall design blends traditional brickwork with contemporary glass and wood elements. The house appears to be a single-story structure with a prominent, light-filled living area.
description: A brick house with a tall main section and a gabled extension featuring exposed wooden rafters and large blue-tinted windows. The design combines traditional brickwork with modern glass and timber elements.
description_view_0: Side view showing the brick main section and the gabled extension with exposed wooden rafters and blue-tinted windows.
description_view_1: View highlighting the open roof structure of the extension, the timber beams, and the symmetrical window arrangement.
description_view_2: Top-down view emphasizing the flat roof of the main section and the open, raftered roof of the extension.
description_view_3: Front view displaying the fully glazed gabled end of the extension and the prominent wooden framing.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical rows with large picture windows
attribute_entryway_design: Main entryway is likely located at the glazed gabled end, featuring large glass doors and no visible porch or steps, emphasizing openness and modern design.
